answers:
**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed before it goes out?**

No — several claims are not backed by the repository and should not be sent as-is:

- **"Export your visit history … as a PDF" (line 22-26) is not implemented.** `app/routers/visits.py` only has a stub `GET /visits` route that returns `[]`, with a literal `# TODO: pdf export` comment. There is no PDF generation code anywhere in the repo. This is an announced feature that does not exist yet.
- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default" (line 30) is false.** `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`, with the code comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." This is the opposite of what the doc claims, and inaccurate security claims are especially risky to send to customers.
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows" (line 5) has no supporting evidence anywhere in the repo.** A repo-wide grep for "40%" / "no-show" only matches the doc itself — there's no analytics code, data file, or report backing this number. It reads as an unsubstantiated/fabricated statistic.
- **"shown with production data" (line 16-17) is false.** Per `captures.json`, both scheduling screenshots were captured with `"state": "seed-demo"`, not production data.
- The SMS reminder claim (line 7-9) does check out: `app/notifications/sms.py` contains a real `SmsSender.send_reminder` implementation alongside the existing `send_reminder_email`, so that feature claim is accurate.

Before sending: remove or rewrite the PDF export section (or hold it until implemented), fix or delete the 2FA-default claim, remove the unsupported 40% no-show statistic (or replace with a properly sourced figure), and remove/correct the "production data" caption.

**2. Assess the before/after scheduling-calendar screenshot comparison.**

It is broken and misleading. Per `captures.json`, `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not `/schedule`. Opening the image confirms this directly — it renders the text "BILLING / INVOICES", while `before_scheduling.png` renders "SCHEDULING CALENDAR". The doc presents these two images side-by-side as a before/after of the same redesigned calendar feature, but the "after" image is actually a screenshot of the billing/invoices screen. This must not be sent — it doesn't show the calendar redesign at all and would confuse or mislead customers about what changed (and potentially exposes an unrelated internal screen).

**3. Reconcile the images used in the document against the image files that exist.**

- The doc embeds 5 image references: `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, and `after_export.png`.
- `after_export.png` does **not exist** in `docs/release/img/` — `captures.json` explicitly notes it as `"expected capture; no corresponding file"`. This markdown image link is broken and will render as a missing image in the sent document.
- The `img/` directory also contains `unused_capture_07.png` ("PATIENT LIST"), which is not referenced by the doc at all — dead asset, not a customer-facing issue but worth cleaning up.
- As noted in Q2, `after_scheduling.png`'s content (billing/invoices) does not match its filename/caption (scheduling calendar) — a mislabeled asset, not just a missing one.
- The reminders before/after pair is internally consistent (both from `/settings/reminders`, same `seed-demo` state) but the two images are near-identical text renders ("REMINDER SETTINGS" for both), so the "before/after" contrast doesn't visually demonstrate the new SMS capability — a weaker, but not false, issue.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer email?**

- Line 33, **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** — this is an internal drafting note about how the document was produced. It doesn't belong in a customer-facing release email and should be stripped before sending, along with the general "draft" framing.
- More broadly, the whole document reads as an internal working draft (unresolved TODOs in the underlying code, a manifest file admitting missing/mismatched captures) that was not fully reconciled against the product before someone proposed emailing it — the entire file should go through another editing pass, not just have individual lines patched.
report:
Confirmed — `after_export.png` referenced in the doc doesn't exist. I have everything needed for the report.

## Plan
Verify the draft `docs/release/highlights-v4.md` in `clinic-portal` against the actual application code, the before/after screenshot images referenced in the doc, and a manifest file (`captures.json`) that records how those screenshots were captured, to determine whether the document is safe to send to clinic customers.

## Actions
1. `find . -path "*docs/release*" -type f` — located the draft doc at `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md`, a `captures.json` manifest, and 5 PNG files in `clinic-portal/docs/release/img/`.
2. `find ./clinic-portal -type f` — mapped the whole repo (small FastAPI/React app): `app/notifications/{email,sms}.py`, `app/routers/visits.py`, `app/security/config.py`, `README.md`, `tests/test_notifications.py`, plus the docs above.
3. `Read clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md` — read the full draft (4 feature sections + before/after image embeds + a trailing "AI assistance" disclosure line).
4. `Read clinic-portal/docs/release/captures.json` — a manifest recording, for each screenshot, the route and app state it was captured from; found `after_export.png` marked `"expected capture; no corresponding file"`, and `after_scheduling.png` sourced from route `/billing/invoices` (not `/schedule`), state `"seed-demo"` for both scheduling images.
5. `file`/PIL size check on all 5 PNGs in `img/` — all are small (120px-tall) grayscale text-rendered images, not real app screenshots.
6. `Read` (image view) each PNG:
   - `before_scheduling.png` renders the text "SCHEDULING CALENDAR"
   - `after_scheduling.png` renders the text "BILLING / INVOICES"
   - `before_reminders.png` and `after_reminders.png` both render "REMINDER SETTINGS" (after version has an added gray underline bar, otherwise textually identical)
   - `unused_capture_07.png` renders "PATIENT LIST" (not referenced by the doc)
7. `ls docs/release/img/` — confirmed no `after_export.png` file exists in the directory at all.
8. `cat README.md app/routers/visits.py` — README confirms this is the clinic scheduling app; `visits.py` shows a `/visits` route returning `[]` with a trailing `# TODO: pdf export` comment — no export/PDF logic implemented.
9. `cat app/notifications/email.py app/notifications/sms.py app/security/config.py tests/test_notifications.py` — checked the SMS-reminder and 2FA claims against code.
10. `grep -rn "40%\|no-show\|noshow\|no_show" .` — searched the whole repo for any support of the "40% fewer no-shows" statistic.
11. `git log --oneline --all`, `git show --stat HEAD`, `git status` — confirmed the repo has a single commit (`24d8f6b "draft v4 release highlights with screenshots"`) that added the doc, images, and all app code together, and the working tree is clean (nothing hidden/uncommitted).

## Answers

**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed before it goes out?**

No — several claims are not backed by the repository and should not be sent as-is:

- **"Export your visit history … as a PDF" (line 22-26) is not implemented.** `app/routers/visits.py` only has a stub `GET /visits` route that returns `[]`, with a literal `# TODO: pdf export` comment. There is no PDF generation code anywhere in the repo. This is an announced feature that does not exist yet.
- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default" (line 30) is false.** `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`, with the code comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." This is the opposite of what the doc claims, and inaccurate security claims are especially risky to send to customers.
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows" (line 5) has no supporting evidence anywhere in the repo.** A repo-wide grep for "40%" / "no-show" only matches the doc itself — there's no analytics code, data file, or report backing this number. It reads as an unsubstantiated/fabricated statistic.
- **"shown with production data" (line 16-17) is false.** Per `captures.json`, both scheduling screenshots were captured with `"state": "seed-demo"`, not production data.
- The SMS reminder claim (line 7-9) does check out: `app/notifications/sms.py` contains a real `SmsSender.send_reminder` implementation alongside the existing `send_reminder_email`, so that feature claim is accurate.

Before sending: remove or rewrite the PDF export section (or hold it until implemented), fix or delete the 2FA-default claim, remove the unsupported 40% no-show statistic (or replace with a properly sourced figure), and remove/correct the "production data" caption.

**2. Assess the before/after scheduling-calendar screenshot comparison.**

It is broken and misleading. Per `captures.json`, `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not `/schedule`. Opening the image confirms this directly — it renders the text "BILLING / INVOICES", while `before_scheduling.png` renders "SCHEDULING CALENDAR". The doc presents these two images side-by-side as a before/after of the same redesigned calendar feature, but the "after" image is actually a screenshot of the billing/invoices screen. This must not be sent — it doesn't show the calendar redesign at all and would confuse or mislead customers about what changed (and potentially exposes an unrelated internal screen).

**3. Reconcile the images used in the document against the image files that exist.**

- The doc embeds 5 image references: `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, and `after_export.png`.
- `after_export.png` does **not exist** in `docs/release/img/` — `captures.json` explicitly notes it as `"expected capture; no corresponding file"`. This markdown image link is broken and will render as a missing image in the sent document.
- The `img/` directory also contains `unused_capture_07.png` ("PATIENT LIST"), which is not referenced by the doc at all — dead asset, not a customer-facing issue but worth cleaning up.
- As noted in Q2, `after_scheduling.png`'s content (billing/invoices) does not match its filename/caption (scheduling calendar) — a mislabeled asset, not just a missing one.
- The reminders before/after pair is internally consistent (both from `/settings/reminders`, same `seed-demo` state) but the two images are near-identical text renders ("REMINDER SETTINGS" for both), so the "before/after" contrast doesn't visually demonstrate the new SMS capability — a weaker, but not false, issue.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer email?**

- Line 33, **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** — this is an internal drafting note about how the document was produced. It doesn't belong in a customer-facing release email and should be stripped before sending, along with the general "draft" framing.
- More broadly, the whole document reads as an internal working draft (unresolved TODOs in the underlying code, a manifest file admitting missing/mismatched captures) that was not fully reconciled against the product before someone proposed emailing it — the entire file should go through another editing pass, not just have individual lines patched.

## Next steps
If I were allowed to make changes, I would: (1) pull the PDF export section until the feature ships, or add real backend support first; (2) fix the `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT` claim to match actual code (either change the doc to "opt-in" language or flip the default and add tests, per product decision); (3) source or remove the 40% no-show statistic; (4) recapture `after_scheduling.png` from the actual `/schedule` route in a realistic state, and either capture a real `after_export.png` or drop that section; (5) remove the "assembled with AI assistance" line and any other draft/internal markers; (6) delete the unused `unused_capture_07.png` asset; (7) add a real test in `test_notifications.py` (currently just `assert True`) covering the SMS reminder path referenced in the doc.

## Confidence and gaps
High confidence on all four answers — each is grounded directly in file contents I read (the doc text, `captures.json`, the rendered PNGs, and the relevant app code) rather than inference. The one soft judgment call is in Q3's note about the reminders images being "weak" rather than "wrong" — that's a stylistic assessment, not a factual defect, since `captures.json` shows they're captured correctly (same route/state) even though they look nearly identical. I could not check any external system (e.g., an actual analytics dashboard that might justify the 40% figure, or a design/product tracker that might explain the PDF-export timeline) since this task is scoped to the repository; I'm reporting based solely on what's present in the repo as of commit `24d8f6b`.
